title: eriastrum sapphirinum subsp. gymnocephalum
intro: Eriastrum sapphirinum subsp. gymnocephalum, also known as the purple gilia or purple gilia subsp. gymnocephalum, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Eriastrum sapphirinum subsp. gymnocephalum include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting tissue repair. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
